notes: Roman Emperor from 367 to 383, noted for his Christian faith and opposition to Arianism.

docs.title: BOOK XXX
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: … in greater security, because he, wise and kindhearted man as he was, loved his young relative with exceeding affection, and brought him up with great care. No one has succeeded in explaining this word.

docs.title: BOOK XXXI
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: … young prince of admirable disposition, eloquent, moderate, warlike, and merciful, rivalling the most admirable of his predecessors, even while the down of youth was still upon his cheeks; the only drawback to his character being that he was sometimes drawn into ridiculous actions, when, in

docs.title: Letter 3
docs.author: Ambrose of Milan
docs.excerpt: To the most merciful Emperors, the Christian and most glorious princes Gratian, Valentinian, and Theodosius — from the Council assembled at Aquileia. Most merciful Emperors, your decrees have ensured that Arian faithlessness [the heresy denying the full divinity of
